title: Hedging-account semantics: rebalance REDUCTIONS open hedges instead of reducing (close_position already fixed)
source: Live demo breach test 2026-05-29: demo is RETAIL_HEDGING (margin_mode=2). close_position fixed (close-by-ticket). Same bug class remains on the rebalance path.
why: On a hedging account, reducing a position via place_order(opposite side) OPENS a hedge, not a reduction. close_position is now fixed (close-by-ticket), but the reconcile/rebalance path that REDUCES a position toward target still issues a plain opposite order -> on hedging it adds a hedge (net exposure ends ~right but position count grows, margin wasted, clutter accumulates each rebalance). The first weekly/daily rebalance that reduces a leg will surface this on the demo.
value_rationale: High: rebalance fidelity on hedging accounts; the Syphonix/hackathon account mode is UNKNOWN, so the layer must handle both netting and hedging correctly before the competition.
scope: Reconcile/rebalance: to REDUCE a net long, close (partially) by ticket(s) rather than opening an opposite order; to flip, close-then-open. Detect account margin_mode (hedging vs netting) and branch.
Ensure get_positions nets correctly per symbol on hedging (multiple tickets per symbol).
Minor: close fill_qty logged in lots while qty logged in base units -- unify the audit-log units.
Test with FakeHedgingMT5 (already added) on the rebalance/reduce path.

history.reason: logged from live breach-test hedging finding

Hedging-safe rebalance implemented: reductions close tickets (no hedge), flips close-then-open, increases add; branched by account margin_mode; get_positions nets per symbol; audit units unified. 1007 tests pass; netting unchanged. Broker owns the seam (reconcile/live_loop untouched).
fix(execution): B0091 hedging-safe rebalance (reduce=close-by-ticket, flip=close+open); gitignore runtime state
